pattern: Group every point-pair by its reduced direction, count pairs of parallel non-collinear segments, then subtract parallelograms once because they are counted by both of their parallel side pairs.
hints: Any pair of parallel sides comes from two segments with the same slope that do not lie on the same line. Reduce each segment's direction to a canonical (dx, dy) so equal slopes collide.
Within one slope, the number of valid parallel side-pairs is all-pairs-of-segments minus the pairs that share a line (collinear segments cannot bound a trapezoid).

A parallelogram has two pairs of parallel sides, so it is counted twice; detect parallelograms by point-pairs sharing a diagonal midpoint (excluding degenerate collinear quadruples) and subtract each once.

edge_inputs.description: two slanted trapezoids from the official example
a parallelogram is counted once, not twice
minimal four points with one parallel pair

stress.description: small random distinct points on a tight grid, mixing slopes and parallelograms
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    target = max(4, min(9, size))
    pts = set()
    while len(pts) < target:
        pts.add((rng.randint(0, 5), rng.randint(0, 5)))
    out = [list(p) for p in pts]
    rng.shuffle(out)
    return [out]
stress.note: small because the oracle enumerates all 4-point subsets
